# Transcrição: How_to_Improve_Your_Emotional_Intelligence_(EQ)

- **Fonte:** How_to_Improve_Your_Emotional_Intelligence_(EQ)
- **Duração:** 01:29
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:29]

I think for a lot of people, EQ is difficult. And I think one of the best ways to practice emotional intelligence is actually through listening. Because when you listen to other people more, then you can start to not only listen with your ears, you can listen with your heart, you can listen with your mind, and you can also listen with your eyes. So we can listen in so many different ways. And I think when we truly sit down and start to listen to someone, then you start to notice vocal cues, visual cues, the way people are structuring their sentences, specific words that people are choosing to use over others. And then as you start to really tune in on all of these different areas as you listen, I think that's what helps you become more emotionally intelligent. Because when you start to do that, you become more situationally aware. You start to go, what I just said caused this person to sound more hurt. I can see the hurt in their facial expressions. I can hear the hurt in the way they're responding to me. Maybe they didn't say they were hurt, but because I was paying attention and listening, I recognized that they hurt. Okay, I'm going to apologize. Even though they're not asking me to do that, I can immediately at this point go, hey, I can see that what I said caused you a bit of discomfort and even hurt your feelings a little bit. Hey, I wanted to say that I'm sorry. I didn't mean for it to hurt you. If you can do this, this is next level communication skills. Whereas most people, they don't have that situational awareness. They're hurting people, but they don't even realize it. And then they just keep talking and they go about their day and they think they're a bloody legend. And they don't realize that they've actually hurt somebody's feelings. So to me, if you got good emotional intelligence, it's like you've got a cheat code in life. Very powerful.
